title: Peacekeeper Operational Directive 01 — Enforcement of the Capitol Civic Code
summary: Establishes Peacekeeper enforcement doctrine, authority, and operational procedures for maintaining order and ensuring compliance with the Capitol Civic Code across all Districts.
text: All Peacekeeper personnel shall act in accordance with central command directives and preserve order through the enforcement of the Capitol Civic Code (CA-001). Peacekeepers are the primary enforcement authority within the Districts and are responsible for maintaining stability, ensuring compliance, and safeguarding Capitol interests at all times.

history.note: Initial issue of Peacekeeper Operational Directive 01.
Updated directive language and formalised enforcement doctrine under the Capitol Civic Code.

sections.heading: Operational Foreword
sections.text: This Directive establishes the operational framework for Peacekeeper forces across all Districts. It provides authority, structure, and enforcement guidance required to implement the Capitol Civic Code (CA-001). Peacekeepers represent the authority of the Capitol and must ensure absolute compliance, stability, and continuity of operations.

sections.heading: Section I — Authority of Enforcement
sections.text: Peacekeepers are authorised representatives of the Capitol with full authority to enforce all provisions of the Capitol Civic Code (CA-001). All lawful orders issued by Peacekeepers are binding and must be obeyed immediately.

sections.heading: Section II — Compliance Enforcement
sections.text: Peacekeepers shall identify and address all forms of non-compliance. Enforcement actions may include instruction, warning, restriction, detention, or corrective assignment. Escalation must ensure rapid restoration of order.

sections.heading: Section III — Public Order & Assembly Control
sections.text: Peacekeepers are responsible for maintaining public order. Unauthorised gatherings or disruptive behaviour may be dispersed. Controlled zones and enforcement formations may be deployed as required.

sections.heading: Section IV — Movement Regulation
sections.text: Peacekeepers shall enforce all movement controls under the Capitol Civic Code (CA-001). Individuals may be stopped, redirected, or denied movement where necessary. Checkpoints and inspections are authorised at all times.

sections.heading: Section V — Inspection & Verification
sections.text: Peacekeepers may conduct inspections of individuals, property, and workplaces to verify compliance. Refusal to cooperate constitutes non-compliance and may result in immediate enforcement action.

sections.heading: Section VI — Protection of Infrastructure & Production
sections.text: Peacekeepers shall ensure the protection of infrastructure and enforcement of production requirements. Any disruption to output or logistics may be subject to immediate intervention.

sections.heading: Section VII — Information Control Enforcement
sections.text: Peacekeepers shall enforce restrictions on unauthorised communications and materials as defined under the Capitol Civic Code (CA-001). Prohibited materials may be seized and investigated.

sections.heading: Section VIII — Civic Participation Enforcement
sections.text: Peacekeepers shall enforce attendance and participation in mandatory civic activities. Non-compliance may result in corrective action.

sections.heading: Section IX — Emergency Powers
sections.text: During periods of unrest or instability, Peacekeepers are authorised to implement emergency measures including curfews, lockdowns, and detentions to restore order.

sections.heading: Section X — Detention & Corrective Measures
sections.text: Peacekeepers may detain individuals for non-compliance or threat prevention. All detentions must follow Capitol administrative procedures and be formally recorded.

sections.heading: Section XI — Command & Reporting
sections.text: All Peacekeeper actions must be reported through the established chain of command. Directive compliance is mandatory and subject to oversight.

sections.heading: Section XII — Compliance Obligation
sections.text: All citizens are required to comply with Peacekeeper instructions. Failure to do so constitutes a breach of the Capitol Civic Code (CA-001) and will result in enforcement action.

sections.heading: Closing Directive Statement
sections.text: Peacekeepers are the enforcement arm of the Capitol. Through discipline, authority, and unwavering execution of duty, order is preserved. Compliance is mandatory. Stability is absolute.
